column1: # eg false-positive query corpus. Columns: label<TAB>pattern<TAB>flags
# Tuned for a Linux kernel checkout but generic enough for any C-heavy corpus.
# --- exact literals: rare, medium, common, long ---
# --- gap queries (island co-occurrence; FP floor expected) ---
# --- escaped control chars in source text ---
# --- multiple patterns / regression shapes from codesearch tests ---
# --- reviewer-derived: open repetition seams, look contexts, wide pairs ---
# --- verifier-semantics flags: smart case, no-unicode, multiline, crlf ---
# --- round-2 reviewer findings: budget gate, cover accounting, min=2 seams ---
# --- audit sentinels: numeric/hex/version (largest non-floor FP class) ---
# --- audit sentinels: smart case and non-ASCII ---
# --- known-unsupported (should stay graceful, recorded not crashed) ---
# --- production-readiness additions: numeric/hex/version (largest non-floor FP class) ---
# --- nullable anchor bridges: optional indentation/trailing space can sever sentinel-to-literal seams ---
# --- wide mixed classes: ASCII plus Unicode boundary sets can collapse and admit bare literals ---
# Branch-correlation sentinel: a merged wide class must not let different class bytes satisfy opposite seams.
# --- structured IDs: large bounded classes should stay constrained without plan blowups ---
# --- harness sentinels: transparent scan fallback must not produce negative FP metrics ---
# Outer Unicode word-boundary edge: positive gram plans need indexed boundary context to reject word-prefixed occurrences.
# --- simple human queries: plain words and phrases a person types ---
# --- prose phrases from comments and docs ---
# --- zero-match probes: candidates admitted for absent strings are pure FP ---
